title: CI: route x86/Linux jobs to [self-hosted, azure-ephemeral] runners
description: Per Harry's cross-project directive (2026-06-28): bring CI onto the new azure-ephemeral self-hosted GitHub runners (x86-capable) to unstick the starved/flaky existing fleet. feedback-cli has NO macOS/darwin jobs — all 4 jobs (ci.yml build-test; release.yml resolve/test/publish) are linux-x86 nix jobs — so all route to [self-hosted, azure-ephemeral]. Heads-up: runners need Nix (confirmed on base image) AND the SSH key for the private mcp-cli flake input, else the test/build jobs fail at private-crate fetch. Sequencing: don't trigger before runners are live (cancel any queued run).
creator: aurora:checkout:aurora-feedback-cli-feedback-cli-dev-aur-0

title: End-to-end webhook -> caco bead/perf integration test
description:
Once the caco webhook ingestion endpoint exists (see bd-4fe7e8), add an end-to-end test/harness proving feedback-cli's WebhookSink drives the intended caco actions:
- an error/exception FeedbackEvent POSTed to the caco webhook creates a bead,
- a perf FeedbackEvent records a `caco log perf` entry,
- auth (bearer token) and non-2xx handling behave correctly.

feedback-cli already has a std-only one-shot HTTP server unit test (webhook_sink_posts_event_with_auth_header) asserting the POST + Authorization header + payload; this bead is the real caco-side round trip. Depends on bd-4fe7e8 (caco webhook integration).

UNBLOCKED 2026-06-22: dependency bd-4fe7e8 is closed; the feedback-cli side is fully landed (caco_bead webhook payload mode + CACOPHONY_<PROJECT>_WEBHOOK_TOKEN convention, README "Wiring to a caco webhook" recipe, examples/report.rs). The caco webhook ingress already exists (POST /hooks/<scope>/<id>, bead handler, bd-bbe6a1). REMAINING (operator/cacophony-gated, not solo-completable from a feedback-cli worker): configure an actual caco webhook `bead` hook + bearer token, then run the live e2e proving a posted FeedbackEvent (payload=caco_bead) creates a bead and a perf event records caco perf. Ready to pick up once a hook is configured.
creator: aurora:feedback-cli:aurora-feedback-cli-feedback-cli-dev-aur-0

title: CI: make azure-ephemeral jobs secretless-safe (SSH-key secret + gh-via-nix)
description: Follow-up to bd-07aba5 (azure-ephemeral routing). The azure-ephemeral pool is SECRETLESS, NOT on tailnet, and only has Nix on the image (no bare cargo/gh/etc.). Per Harry's two follow-up directives: (1) inject required secrets as GitHub Actions secrets; (2) get toolchains from Nix, not a custom runner image. feedback-cli changes: ci.yml build-test + release.yml test now inject a read-only deploy key for the private mcp-cli git+ssh input from secret PRIVATE_DEPS_SSH_KEY (soft when absent, so a key-bearing self-hosted runner still works); release.yml publish now runs `gh` via `nix run nixpkgs#gh` (gh is not on the image). Build/test were already nix-native (nix build / nix flake check), and cargo publish was already `nix develop --command`, so no bare-toolchain jobs remain. REMAINING OPERATOR STEPS: (a) add feedback-cli to the runner pool repos in sandbox/ops/terraform/runners.nix + redeploy; (b) set the PRIVATE_DEPS_SSH_KEY secret (deploy key with read access to harryaskham/mcp-cli) — confirm the cross-project secret name so it's consistent.
creator: aurora:checkout:aurora-feedback-cli-feedback-cli-dev-aur-0

title: Feature-gate the webhook (ureq/TLS) dependency behind a default-on `webhook` feature
description:
feedback-cli pulls ureq (rustls/ring TLS) unconditionally for the WebhookSink. Consumers that only use the stderr / caco_cli / disabled strategies (likely common across the CLI suite now embedding feedback-cli, e.g. hello-world-cli) still pay that compile-time + binary cost.

Make it opt-out, non-breaking:
- Cargo.toml: `ureq` becomes optional; `[features] default = ["webhook"]`, `webhook = ["dep:ureq"]`.
- Gate only the ureq HTTP send in WebhookSink::record behind cfg(feature="webhook"); without the feature, record() returns FeedbackError::Config("webhook reporting requires the `webhook` feature"). Keep WebhookSink/WebhookConfig/WebhookPayload/ReportStrategy::Webhook always present so the config schema is stable.
- Gate webhook-exercising tests (unit webhook_sink_posts_event_with_auth_header + tests/caco_webhook_e2e.rs) behind the feature.

Validate both configs: default (webhook on) full test+clippy green; `--no-default-features` build + clippy -D warnings green (no dead_code). Default features unchanged, so templates and other current consumers are unaffected.
creator: aurora:checkout:aurora-feedback-cli-feedback-cli-dev-aur-0

title: Multi-platform drop-in feedback clients (web/ ios/ android/) — same webhook + caco_bead contract
description: Just like feedback-cli is a drop-in for Rust CLIs, add platform subprojects so cacophony apps can submit feedback->beads from web, iPhone/macOS, and Android. Same hooks: POST FeedbackEvent (event payload) or to_caco_bead (title/description/type/priority/labels) JSON to a caco webhook bead hook with Bearer token. Contract mirrors src/lib.rs: kind error|exception|perf|info; type bug for error/exception else task; priority critical1/warning3/info4/error|none2; labels ["feedback","kind:<k>",...]. Subprojects: web/ (TS), ios/ (Swift; macOS too), android/ (Kotlin). FFI over the Rust core optional later; v1 = tiny native HTTP clients, dependency-light. Drop-in = one file + README each.
creator: aurora:checkout:aurora-feedback-cli-feedback-cli-dev-aur-0

title: Integrate feedback-cli webhook strategy with caco webhooks (once caco webhook ingestion lands)
description:
feedback-cli ships a `webhook` ReportStrategy (WebhookSink): it POSTs each FeedbackEvent as JSON to a configured URL with an optional bearer token (inline `token` or `token_env`). The CLI is a webhook *consumer*; the receiver decides what to do.

This bead tracks wiring that end-to-end to caco once caco gains a webhook ingestion endpoint that turns posted FeedbackEvents into the right caco action:
- error / exception events -> create a bead (and/or `caco log error`),
- perf events -> `caco log perf`,
- info -> `caco log error --severity info/warning` or drop.

BLOCKED ON (external): the caco webhook ingestion feature in the cacophony project is not yet landed (cannot be referenced as an in-project dependency from feedback-cli). When it lands, deliver:
1. a documented project-config recipe selecting the webhook strategy (URL + token_env) pointed at the caco webhook,
2. the agreed FeedbackEvent JSON -> caco payload mapping (kind/severity/labels/fields/metric),
3. an example config + README section, and a smoke check.

Payload shape today is the serde form of feedback_cli::FeedbackEvent (kind, component, summary, detail, severity, labels, fields, fingerprint, project, metric, timestamp_unix_ms).

PROGRESS (landed 7c62d5c, 2026-06-18): the webhook payload contract is now documented and shipped — README "Webhook payload contract" section (method/auth/headers + per-field table + suggested caco receiver mapping) plus a runnable examples/report.rs that dumps the exact error/perf payloads. This is the reviewable spec for the caco webhook receiver to parse. STILL OPEN / blocked on the external caco webhook ingestion feature: deliverables (1) project-config recipe pointing the webhook strategy at the caco webhook, (2) agreed FeedbackEvent->caco action mapping confirmed on the caco side, (3) example + smoke check (tracked separately by bd-08b0cf).
creator: aurora:feedback-cli:aurora-feedback-cli-feedback-cli-dev-aur-0

title: Add an opt-in panic hook (panics -> exception feedback events)
description:
The original feedback-cli vision was to "hook into exceptions etc for bead creation". Today feedback-cli only reports exceptions when the host CLI explicitly calls Reporter::report_error / report. There is no automatic exception hook, so an unhandled panic produces no feedback event/bead.

Add an opt-in panic hook:
- `install_panic_hook(config: FeedbackConfig)` (free fn): builds a Reporter, takes the existing panic hook, and sets a hook that reports each panic as a FeedbackKind::Exception event (component "panic", summary = panic message, fields: location file:line:col, thread) via the configured strategy, then chains to the previous hook so default panic output/abort behaviour is preserved.
- Factor the mapping into pure, testable helpers: panic_payload_message(&dyn Any) -> String and panic_feedback_event(message, location) -> FeedbackEvent. Unit-test those (the global hook itself is thin and not unit-tested to avoid global-state races).
- Reporter is Send+Sync+'static (sink is Send+Sync), so it can move into the panic hook closure.
- README note; no new deps (std::panic/std::any). Feature-independent.

This makes "panics -> caco beads/feedback" automatic for CLIs that opt in, completing the exception-hooking part of the vision. Additive, opt-in, non-breaking.
creator: aurora:checkout:aurora-feedback-cli-feedback-cli-dev-aur-0

title: Add an automated cross-client caco_bead parity guard (Rust/web/ios/android)
description:
Observed during the bd-fb6e3a session (drained-board self-audit): feedback-cli ships FOUR hand-written encodings of the same caco_bead mapping — Rust (src/lib.rs FeedbackEvent::to_caco_bead), web (web/feedback.ts), iOS (ios/Feedback.swift), Android (android/Feedback.kt). The README promises they "mirror ... exactly", but nothing guards it: scripts/doctor.sh only checks Rust/CI conventions, and there is no TS/Swift/Kotlin toolchain wired in CI. The web field-ordering drift fixed in bd-fb6e3a (insertion-order vs the Rust BTreeMap sorted order) would have been caught by such a guard.

Suggested shape (pick the lightest that fits CI): a small fixture of representative FeedbackEvents -> expected caco_bead JSON, asserted against each client. node 26 is already available on the runners and can run the .ts client directly via type-stripping (validated this session). A pure-node harness could at minimum cover Rust (via a tiny serialized-fixture compare) + web; Swift/Kotlin would need their toolchains, so they could stay manual/checklist if too heavy.

Lower-effort alternative: a doctor.sh check that greps each client for the key mapping invariants (type bug/task split, priority 1/3/4/2, the feedback + kind:<k> base labels, sorted-field iteration) and warns on divergence.

Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-fb6e3a session.
creator: aurora:checkout:aurora-feedback-cli-feedback-cli-dev-aur-0

title: Add a --no-default-features CI guard (nix flake check) for the webhook feature
description:
After bd-2131bb made `ureq`/the webhook strategy a default-on `webhook` cargo feature, CI (ci.yml runs `nix flake check`) only exercises the default-features build. The `--no-default-features` path (webhook off, ureq dropped) has no CI guard and could regress unnoticed.

Add a CI guard: define a `checks.no-default-features` in flake.nix (a second buildRustPackage with `buildNoDefaultFeatures = true; doCheck = true;`, ideally by factoring the shared package args into a small `let` helper to avoid duplication). `nix flake check` then covers both. cargo already proves the path locally (build + clippy -D warnings + 15 non-webhook tests green, ureq absent from the dep tree); this just guards it in CI.

Deferred from the bd-2131bb session because validating a new nix check needs a full sandbox build, which was ill-advised during the fleet nix-update/high-load window. Low priority polish. Filed via reflect-session from the bd-2131bb session.
creator: aurora:checkout:aurora-feedback-cli-feedback-cli-dev-aur-0
spoken_name: add no default features ci guard

title: CI: fetch public mcp-cli over HTTPS (no SSH key/secret needed on secretless runners)
description: Discovered while trying to unblock feedback-cli CI on the secretless azure-ephemeral pool: mcp-cli is actually PUBLIC (gh visibility=public; clean unauthenticated https ls-remote works). The flake comment claiming "Private repo, fetched over SSH" was stale. So feedback-cli needs NO deploy key and NO PRIVATE_DEPS_SSH_KEY secret — just fetch the public dep over HTTPS. Changes: flake.nix mcp-cli input git+ssh -> git+https (+ flake.lock re-locked to rev 2e4efc1); removed the SSH-key-injection + https->ssh git-auth steps from ci.yml/release.yml (keyless minimum); fixed the stale README ssh/private note. Verified locally: `nix build .#feedback-cli` and `nix flake check` both PASS keyless (no ssh agent, no insteadOf rewrite). This eliminates one of the two CI prerequisites; only the mono pool-membership bead remains. Reverts the now-unnecessary PRIVATE_DEPS_SSH_KEY work from bd-142533.
creator: aurora:checkout:aurora-feedback-cli-feedback-cli-dev-aur-0

title: Optional non-blocking webhook delivery (don't stall the CLI on reporting)
description:
WebhookSink::record POSTs synchronously via ureq, so reporting an event blocks the calling thread until the webhook responds (or times out, default 30s). For a CLI on a slow/unreachable webhook this can noticeably stall the tool, and the panic hook reports inline during unwind.

Consider a non-blocking option: e.g. a background delivery thread + bounded queue (drop/という coalesce on overflow), or a `blocking: bool` config knob, so feedback never stalls the host CLI. Keep it std-only if feasible (a single worker thread + std::sync::mpsc), and keep the synchronous path as default for simplicity/determinism. Trade-off: at-most-once delivery vs. blocking. Filed via reflect-session from the overnight feedback-cli session.
creator: aurora:checkout:aurora-feedback-cli-feedback-cli-dev-aur-0

title: Wire feedback-cli into the templates project (with templates-dev-0)
description:
Add feedback-cli as an ecosystem dependency in the `templates` project, mirroring how mcp-cli / updatable-cli / kittui are wired:
- a `feedback-cli` flake input (`git+ssh://git@github.com/harryaskham/feedback-cli?ref=main`, flake = false) plus a `[patch."https://github.com/harryaskham/feedback-cli"]` redirect in the template flake.nix,
- a `feedback-cli = { git = "https://github.com/harryaskham/feedback-cli", branch = "main" }` dep in the template Cargo.toml(s),
- mount the reporting surface: build a FeedbackConfig from the generated project's config and register the MCP tools via `feedback_cli::register_feedback_tools`, and/or report tool errors with `Reporter::report_error`.

Coordinate with templates-dev-0 (owns the templates project); this worker is scoped to feedback-cli and cannot edit the templates checkout directly. Candidate targets: hello-world-cli (binary template, alongside updatable-cli self-update) and optionally hello-world-lib.
creator: aurora:feedback-cli:aurora-feedback-cli-feedback-cli-dev-aur-0

title: [operator-action] Configure a caco webhook bead hook + run feedback-cli live smoke
description:
feedback-cli's webhook->caco-bead path is fully implemented and proven end-to-end against a faithful mock of caco-daemon's bead webhook handler (tests/caco_webhook_e2e.rs). The only remaining validation needs operator/cacophony config that a feedback-cli worker cannot provision.

OPERATOR STEP:
1. Configure a caco webhook `bead` hook (webhooks: section in caco config) for a test project, e.g. served at POST /hooks/<project>/feedback, with a bearer token.
2. Set the matching token on the feedback-cli side via the convention CACOPHONY_<PROJECT>_WEBHOOK_TOKEN (or CACOPHONY_WEBHOOK_TOKEN), or strategy.webhook.token_env.
3. Point a feedback-cli config at it: strategy { type: webhook, url: https://<node-or-funnel>/hooks/<project>/feedback, payload: caco_bead } and emit a FeedbackEvent::error and ::perf; confirm a bead is created (bug) and the perf event is recorded.

Recipe + payload contract: feedback-cli README "Wiring to a caco webhook" and examples/report.rs. This is the live counterpart to the closed bd-08b0cf mock e2e.

HARNESS READY (2026-06-24): the feedback-cli side is now a one-command run. examples/smoke.rs (landed on main) POSTs a real error + perf FeedbackEvent through the webhook (payload=caco_bead). Once the caco bead hook + per-hook token exist, run:
  FEEDBACK_WEBHOOK_URL=https://<host>/hooks/<project>/<hook-id> FEEDBACK_PROJECT=<project> CACOPHONY_<PROJECT>_WEBHOOK_TOKEN=<token> cargo run --example smoke
then check `caco bd list --project <project>` for the created bug bead. Operator decision (2026-06-24): use a specific per-hook token (hook.token), not the section/node token; feedback-cli sends Authorization: Bearer <token> and resolves it from the convention env var or FEEDBACK_WEBHOOK_TOKEN_ENV. Remaining: operator configures the hook + token; then run the harness.
creator: aurora:checkout:aurora-feedback-cli-feedback-cli-dev-aur-0

title: Cut feedback-cli v0.1.0 release (tag / optional crates.io publish)
description:
feedback-cli is mission-complete on origin/main (webhook/caco_cli/file/stderr/disabled strategies, mcp registrar, token convention, panic hook, feature-gate, CI guards, tests, docs) and consumed by the hello-world-cli template. It has a release.yml + scripts/release.sh + `nix run .#release` flow but has never been tagged/released.

Cut feedback-cli v0.1.0: `nix run .#release -- patch/minor/0.1.0` (bumps Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock/flake.nix, commits, pushes v* tag -> release.yml creates the GitHub Release and, if CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN is set, `cargo publish`). Decide whether to publish to crates.io or keep it a git-tagged ecosystem crate (consumers use it via git like mcp-cli/updatable-cli today). Filed via reflect-session from the overnight feedback-cli session.
creator: aurora:checkout:aurora-feedback-cli-feedback-cli-dev-aur-0

title: Add a `file` reporting strategy (durable JSON-lines local sink)
description:
feedback-cli's tagline is "route ... events to a configurable strategy (caco webhook, caco CLI, or a local sink)", but the only local sink is `stderr`. A durable file sink is the natural local option: a CLI appends JSON-lines feedback to a path that's later collected/shipped/tailed.

Add a `file` reporting strategy:
- `FileConfig { path: String, append: bool (default true) }`.
- `FileSink` impl FeedbackSink: write `event.to_json()` + "\n" to the path (create parent dirs; append by default, truncate if append=false). describe() = "file <path>".
- `ReportStrategy::File(FileConfig)` variant; `build_sink` wires it; `strategy_name` returns "file".
- Tests: append two events to a temp path, assert two JSON lines; round-trip a config with {"type":"file","path":...}.
- README "Reporting strategies" row + a note; examples/report.rs optional.
- No new deps (std::fs only); works in both default and --no-default-features builds (no webhook feature needed).

Additive, non-breaking; default strategy stays stderr.
creator: aurora:checkout:aurora-feedback-cli-feedback-cli-dev-aur-0

title: web client: sort caco_bead footer fields by key to match Rust/iOS/Android contract
description:
The README promises the web/ios/android drop-in clients "mirror FeedbackEvent::to_caco_bead exactly". They diverged on field ordering inside the caco_bead description footer:

- Rust contract: FeedbackEvent.fields is a BTreeMap<String,String>, so to_caco_bead iterates fields in sorted key order (src/lib.rs).
- iOS (ios/Feedback.swift): sorts via .sorted(by: { $0.key < $1.key }).
- Android (android/Feedback.kt): sorts via .toSortedMap().
- web (web/feedback.ts): used Object.entries(e.fields) — INSERTION order, not sorted.

Result: a multi-field event renders an inconsistent (insertion-ordered) footer on the web client vs the sorted footer everywhere else, breaking the "mirrors exactly" contract.

Fix: web/feedback.ts toCacoBead now sorts fields by key (Object.entries(...).sort(([a],[b]) => a<b?-1:a>b?1:0)) before joining the footer. Functional check (node 26 type-strip): fields {zeta,alpha,mike} now render alpha/mike/zeta. Scoped to toCacoBead, where order is visible in the flattened description string; toEvent emits a JSON object where key order is not semantically significant, so it is intentionally left as-is.

Filed and fixed by the feedback-cli dev worker during a drained-board self-audit.
creator: aurora:checkout:aurora-feedback-cli-feedback-cli-dev-aur-0
